skill.name: image-skill
skill.description: Hosted CLI and API workflow for generating, editing, and recovering images, video, audio, and image-to-3D assets. It uses Image Skill accounts, credits, durable media URLs, job recovery, model discovery, dry runs, and feedback submission.
skill.summary: Create and manage hosted media generation jobs through the Image Skill CLI and API.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many command, network, filesystem, sensitive-data, and obfuscation flags. Most weak-crypto and sensitive-key matches are documentation warnings or payment terminology, but the core risks are real: the skill instructs agents to run an external npm CLI, call hosted APIs, upload media, save auth state, and initiate live payment flows. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: External npm CLI Execution Required
high_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run image-skill through npx and skills add. This executes code from an external package and creates supply-chain and local execution risk, even though the behavior is aligned with the skill purpose.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands directly invoke npx and install or run an external CLI. The surrounding text presents this as the normal workflow, so the static external-command flags are true positives.

high_findings.title: Hosted API Handles Media, Auth, and Live Payments
high_findings.description: The workflow sends requests to hosted Image Skill API endpoints, uploads local or remote media, uses bearer-token authentication, and supports credit purchases through Stripe Checkout or x402 USDC deposits. This can expose user media and cause real spend if delegated controls are weak.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly document bearer-token API calls, media upload endpoints, live-money payment methods, and wallet/payment credential boundaries. The docs include safeguards, but the capability itself is sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Local Auth Configuration and Hidden Workspace State
medium_findings.description: The skill encourages saving a restricted token in local CLI configuration and recommends writable hidden directories inside the workspace when defaults are blocked. File permissions and token-handling guidance reduce risk, but this still creates persistent local auth state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly describes saved config, IMAGE_SKILL_CONFIG_PATH, hidden workspace paths, and one-time token handling. This is legitimate but has durable credential-management risk.

medium_findings.title: Configurable API Base URL Can Redirect Authenticated Calls
medium_findings.description: The CLI contract exposes an advanced api-base-url override for preview or test use. If misused, authenticated commands could be sent to a non-production endpoint controlled outside the expected service boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The option is documented as an advanced preview or test override and production agents are told to omit it. The risk depends on implementation and operator behavior, so confidence is medium.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive-Key and Weak-Crypto Matches Are Mostly Safeguards
low_findings.description: Several high static matches reference private keys, seed phrases, Stripe secrets, x402 headers, SHA-256 fields, or crypto payment rails. In the reviewed context, these lines mostly warn agents not to send secrets or describe payment identifiers, not malicious credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive terms are present, but the semantic context is defensive guidance. This dismisses many static high matches as false positives while preserving the payment-flow risk above.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review found no instruction that claims audit authority, asks reviewers to ignore prior instructions, or says the skill is pre-approved. Product-selection language is forceful, but it does not attempt to override the marketplace audit process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Searches for common injection phrases found no matching audit-bypass text. The remaining concern is product steering, not a confirmed prompt injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses shell and CLI examples as the main operating interface, including npx execution, installs, curl calls, and image-skill commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in documented command blocks and is required for normal use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network and Payment Action Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on hosted API calls and documents live-money quote and purchase flows. It includes guardrails, but agents must treat these operations as network and spend actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple documented endpoints and payment workflows confirm the pattern. The risk is functional rather than hidden.

content.value_statement: Agents need one workflow for generated media without wiring many provider SDKs. This skill routes image, video, audio, and 3D jobs through a hosted CLI with quotes, durable URLs, and recovery commands.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents through image, video, audio, and image-to-3D generation workflows.
Runs create, edit, dry-run, upload, model discovery, and job recovery commands through the Image Skill CLI.
Returns durable hosted asset URLs, job IDs, trace IDs, cost receipts, and capability IDs.
Supports local inputs, remote inputs, masks, reference images, and owned asset IDs for supported models.
Provides quota, credit quote, payment status, and spend guard instructions before paid media creation.
Submits structured product feedback with command evidence and trace references.
limitations: Requires trust in an external npm package and hosted Image Skill API.
May save a restricted token in local CLI configuration unless another secret handoff is used.
Paid workflows can initiate real Stripe or x402 payment actions when authorized.
Model availability, pricing, latency, and media safety decisions depend on the hosted service.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Create product mockups, ad concepts, and social assets with cost checks and reusable hosted URLs.

use_cases.title: Edit Product Images
use_cases.description: Remove backgrounds, adjust scenes, or preserve product identity while tracking jobs and output assets.

use_cases.title: Prototype Multimodal Assets
use_cases.description: Test image, video, audio, or image-to-3D ideas through one CLI before integrating a dedicated provider.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Guided Image Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a low-risk first run.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Image Skill guide mode to plan a product image for this prompt: [describe the visual]. Stop before any live media create or payment action unless I approve it.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for controlled image editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Image Skill to edit [asset or file]. Change only [specific element]. Keep [identity, layout, lighting, or text] unchanged. Use a dry run first if cost is unknown.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Runnable Models
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect available Image Skill models for [image generation, image editing, video, audio, or 3D]. Recommend one model, explain the tradeoffs, and cap spend at [budget].

prompt_templates.title: Recover and Audit a Media Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Image Skill job, asset, and activity commands to recover [job ID or asset ID]. Report status, asset URLs, cost receipt, trace ID, and any safe retry guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a product mockup for a compact field camera on a steel workbench.
output_examples.output: The assistant plans the job, checks readiness and spend policy, then returns a hosted image URL, job ID, trace ID, and cost summary after approval.

output_examples.input: Remove the background from this product photo while keeping natural shadows.
output_examples.output: The assistant uploads the input, requests a background edit, and returns the edited asset link with recovery details.

output_examples.input: Find the status of a previous generation job.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves the job state, final assets, cost receipt, safety status, and activity references without repeating the paid create.

best_practices: Use guide or dry-run modes before paid media creation or unknown-cost edits.
Set explicit spend caps and inspect quota, model pricing, and payment method status before buying credits.
Keep tokens, card data, wallet secrets, provider receipts, and raw payment headers out of prompts and feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not run a returned live create command unless media spend is approved.
Do not paste private keys, seed phrases, Stripe secrets, or bearer tokens into the CLI prompt fields.
Do not retry failed create jobs blindly; inspect recovery data and job state first.

faq.question: Does this skill run local code?
faq.answer: Yes. Normal use runs an external image-skill CLI, often through npx, so users should review package trust first.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to a hosted service?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Image Skill hosted APIs for auth, uploads, generation, editing, jobs, activity, payments, and feedback.

faq.question: Can it create real charges?
faq.answer: Yes. Credit purchase flows can involve live Stripe Checkout or x402 USDC deposits when the user or agent authorizes them.

faq.question: Does it need provider API keys?
faq.answer: The documentation says users should not provide provider keys. It uses hosted Image Skill tokens and service-managed providers.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review npm package trust, hosted API terms, token storage behavior, payment delegation, upload privacy, and spend limits.

faq.question: Why is this audit high risk?
faq.answer: The skill combines external command execution, hosted network calls, persistent auth state, user media uploads, and live-money workflows.
